name: dev-loop
description: Five autonomous agents — PM, QA, Dev, Sweep, and Reflect — that run a software-development loop coordinated entirely through Linear ticket state. PM proposes and verifies features from a strategy doc; QA hunts and re-tests bugs; Dev pulls Todo tickets in priority order, implements, self-reviews, ships, and smoke-checks prod (auto-revert on a break); Sweep is a lifecycle janitor that fixes tickets falling through the cracks (missing/wrong owner labels, orphaned In Progress); Reflect is a daily retrospective that studies the loop's own behavior and curates the per-operator lessons.md from recurring evidence (drafting, never auto-applying, structural changes). A one-time `init` setup command (not a loop agent) bootstraps a product into the loop with a DETECT → MAP → ASSEMBLE → LOAD flow — it detects the project shape (greenfield / brownfield / adopting; single- or multi-repo), read-only-maps a brownfield codebase into a fixed-heading PM doc-base, runs a greenfield strategy interview when there is no code yet, gathers config (incl. extra repos), provisions Linear labels/project (and one `repo:<name>` label per repo when multi-repo), scaffolds the strategy doc/test env/runtime files, optionally adopts named pre-existing human tickets with per-ticket operator confirmation, and prints a readiness checklist. The loop coordinates one or many repos: tickets target a repo via a `repo:<name>` label, with per-repo build/branch/deploy resolution (single-repo stays 100% unchanged). Every ticket the system touches carries a `dev-loop` label so it never disturbs the human backlog. Coordination runs through a configurable backend — Linear (default) or a machine-local file board (`backend:"local"`) with the same state machine and protocols.
